edges.candidate: A file whose size is exactly at the max upload limit (e.g. exactly 5 MB), one byte under, and one byte over — is the boundary inclusive or exclusive, and which side is accepted?

A file larger than the size field can represent (e.g. >2 GB where a 32-bit length is used) or a declared Content-Length that overflows/disagrees with the actual byte stream — what is the contract when the size counter overflows or mismatches?

A filename containing multi-byte Unicode, combining/grapheme clusters, or differing normalization forms (NFC vs NFD), plus format detection by magic bytes vs extension (a .png that is actually a JPEG or a polyglot) — whose definition of 'image' and 'name length' governs acceptance?
A zero-byte file, a truncated/0x0-pixel image, or a request with the file field present but null — what is the result for empty/degenerate upload input?

An upload interrupted mid-stream (dropped connection) or two uploads of the profile-picture field submitted in parallel — is a partial upload guaranteed to be discarded and is the final state well-defined?

The same user saves a profile picture twice (re-upload of the identical image, or resubmit of the same save request) — does it replace the existing picture cleanly, or create a duplicate stored object / stale orphan?

Two saves for the same profile race, or the profile page is read while a new image is being written — is there a guarantee against showing a half-written image, a broken link, or a mix of old metadata with new bytes?
